translated: It was right after your solo performance, Sayurin, but...
seeing Kanon-chan's performance really gave us a push.
It made us feel like, "Alright, we're gonna do our best next!"
It was so great.
Actually, Saku came up to talk to me backstage.
I think it was during the rehearsal, but...
I was really nervous, wondering if I'd be okay.
But Saku came over and spoke to me, and it made me so happy.
She told me she was really moved by it.
I was moved every single time! It was amazing.
I was so happy. I thought, "Oh, it's Tomari-chan!"
It really made my day.
I got a lot of energy from you too.
Seeing Sayurin singing Kanon-chan's song...
I could really feel your soul in it.
My soul?
I'm honored.
Thank you.
Thanks for the soul.
I'm glad it reached you.
And the costume too, right?
Doesn't it fit perfectly?
That energetic vibe.
It really does.
Both options were good, and the other candidate was lovely too,
but once it was decided, I realized this one was the perfect fit.
I'm glad. The other one was like a long one-piece dress,
so if we'd gone with that, my movements probably would've been...
a bit more graceful, I think.
If you'd sprinted full speed in that, it would've looked a bit off.
Holding the hem of the dress like a princess going up the stairs.
I might have looked like I was just flailing around,
but it's all about the sneaker laces.
Just like Kanon.
Yeah.
It's in the lyrics of "Kokoro Kirarara" too.
And these colors, blue and orange, and on the back...
the "Superstar" embroidery.
It's so wonderful.
I love that it's on the back.
The fact that Kanon herself can't see it.
It feels like she's carrying it on her shoulders.
Exactly.
It brings a tear to my eye.
It really feels like everyone's attention to detail
came together to make it complete.
I was really happy about it.
Thanks for noticing all those details.
Thank you very much.
Shall we move on to the next one?
Sure.
Next, we have one from Radio name: Kabocha Samurai no Hasshin...
Wait, it's from Riya-san.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
"I attended Day 1 of the 7th Live in Kanagawa.
The visuals for 'Wild Card' on the screen matched the song perfectly,
and the costume had an Alice in Wonderland vibe.
I was completely drawn into that world.
The choreography also incorporated cards, and even though they weren't there,
it really looked like you were flipping and spreading them out."
That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
Next, we have one from Radio name: Yochi-san.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
"Great job on the two days of the 7th Live in Kanagawa.
I was there at the venue for both days.
Saku-chan, dressed in that cute white-based 'Wild Card' costume,
your singing and dancing with perfect facial expressions
was truly Tomari herself.
Since you mentioned the unique dance during the MC,
my friends and I had a great time mimicking it on our way home.
Thank you again for a wonderful live show."
That's the message.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
There it is.
There it is, 'Wild Card'!
